rs4680.name: Val158Met

GG.interpretation: For val158met, your COMT variant is one contributing factor — rapid dopamine breakdown - stress resilient, better working memory under pressure

AG.interpretation: For val158met, your COMT variant is one contributing factor — balanced dopamine levels - moderate stress response
recommendations: Moderate approach to stimulants and stress

AA.interpretation: For val158met, your COMT variant is one contributing factor — slow dopamine breakdown - higher baseline dopamine, sensitive to stress This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
AA.theWhy: Your COMT variant means dopamine clears more slowly, leaving you more sensitive to stress and stimulants
AA.scienceSimplified: Your body's stress-response system stays active longer due to slower dopamine processing

G/A.interpretation: Your COMT genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to val158met. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of COMT in val158met
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

bibliography: Chen J, Song J, Yuan P, et al. Orientation and cellular distribution of membrane-bound catechol-Omethyltransferase in cortical neurons: implications for drug development. J Biol Chem. 2011 Oct
Morris KA, Grace SA, Woods W, et al. The influence of COMT rs4680 on functional connectivity in healthy
adults: A systematic review. Eur J Neurosci. 2020 Oct;52(8):3851-3878.
Munafò MR, Johnstone EC, Guo B, et al. Association of COMT Val108/158Met genotype with smoking
Serrano JM, Banks JB, Fagan TJ, et al. The influence of Val158Met COMT on physiological stress
Smith SB, Reenilä I, Männistö PT, et al. Epistasis between polymorphisms in COMT, ESR1, and GCH1
influences COMT enzyme activity and pain. Pain. 2014 Nov;155(11):2390-9.
rs4680.technical_report:
Among the polymorphisms described in COMT, Val158Met, also known as V158M, c.472,
G>A or rs4680, stands out.G>A or rs4680. This is a common variant, present in 50% of
Caucasians, which produces a change of an amino acid valine (Val) to a methionine (Met) at
codon 158 resulting in a less stable enzyme with three to four times lower reduced activity
compared to non-carriers of the variant (Val/Val genotype). The A allele (Met) is associated
with lower COMT activity resulting in increased dopamine levels at neuronal synapses,
primarily in the prefrontal cortex (Smith et al., 2014). Val158Met and antidepressant response: since the COMT enzyme is involved in the
response to antidepressant treatment, it was initially thought that the GG genotype (Val/Val)
was likely to influence the antidepressant response to selective serotonin reuptake
inhibitors or SSRIs. However, in more recent studies it has not been possible to confirm this
association in different populations so its clinical application is not yet possible. Val158Met and the treatment of low back pain (morphine, opioids, remifentanil and
propranolol): According to several studies, patients with the GG genotype (Val/Val) who
receive treatment with opioids for pain may present a lower response to morphine, opioids,
remifentanil and propranolol compared to patients with the AG (Met/Val) and AA (Met/Met)
genotype. However, more studies are needed and there is no consensus on its usefulness in

 clinical practice. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence a patient's opioid
dose and response. Val158Met and smoking cessation treatment: Dopamine is a neurotransmitter linked to
nicotine addiction. The Val158Met polymorphism has been extensively researched for its influence
on the effectiveness of pharmacological treatments for smoking cessation and its potential
relationship to smoking addiction. Patients with the GG (Val/Val) genotype who are treated
with nicotine replacement therapy may have a lower likelihood of smoking cessation and a
higher risk of relapse compared to patients with the AA (Met/Met) genotype (Munafò et al.,
2008). However, it is suggested that genetic and clinical factors may also influence patient
response to nicotine replacement therapy. Val158Met and human behavior: The "warrior/worrier" model refers to how different
variants of the COMT gene can influence a person's behavior and emotional regulation. This
model primarily examines the Val158Met polymorphism of the COMT gene, which has a
marked effect on the activity of the enzyme. In healthy adults, free of psychiatric or
neurological disorders, the G (Val) allele is associated with lower synaptic dopamine levels
and lower postsynaptic activity resulting in improved stress resilience, a higher pain
threshold and slightly diminished executive cognitive performance. Whereas the presence of
an A (Met) allele predicts a lower pain threshold, increased vulnerability to stress and
anxiety, and greater efficiency in information processing. COMT studies are an active field
of research, and results are sometimes contradictory or not fully replicable because of the
interaction between multiple genes and environmental factors (Morris et al., 2020). Val158Met and disease: multiple studies have linked the Val158Met variant as a possible
risk factor for schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, panic disorder, anxiety, obsessive-compulsive
disorder, eating disorders, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder. Studies suggest that
these conditions can be related to inefficient information processing in the prefrontal
cortex. However, it is important to consider that these disorders are complex and influenced
by multiple risk factors.
rs4680.description:
Gene COMT
The COMT gene is expressed in the brain where it plays a fundamental role in the regulation
of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and is also found in other tissues such as liver,
kidneys and blood where it participates in the control of the levels of certain hormones.

CC.interpretation: For val66met, your BDNF variant is one contributing factor — full brain-derived neurotrophic factor function - efficient activity-dependent release

CT.interpretation: Met carrier - altered activity-dependent BDNF secretion, affects memory consolidation This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

GC.interpretation: For c-1291g, your ADRA2A variant is one contributing factor — altered receptor expression may affect stress and blood pressure regulation

GG.interpretation: May have elevated stress reactivity, higher baseline anxiety This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

AA.interpretation: For 5-httlpr, your SLC6A4 variant is one contributing factor — long allele homozygote - efficient serotonin reuptake, resilience to stress

AG.interpretation: For 5-httlpr, your SLC6A4 variant is one contributing factor — heterozygous - moderate serotonin transporter activity

GG.interpretation: Short allele homozygote - lower serotonin reuptake, elevated anxiety vulnerability This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

CC.interpretation: For his452tyr, your HTR2A variant is one contributing factor — standard serotonin 2a receptor function

CT.interpretation: For his452tyr, your HTR2A variant is one contributing factor — may affect serotonin signaling and psychiatric medication response This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Some atypical antipsychotics may be more effective

TT.interpretation: May notably affect serotonin signaling, altered medication response This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Discuss with before starting serotonin medications

recommendations: Standard response to CNS drugs metabolized by CYP2D6

TC.interpretation: lower metabolism of some psychiatric medications This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: May have altered response to certain antidepressants, opioids

CC.interpretation: notably lower CYP2D6 activity This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

GG.interpretation: For taq1a, your ANKK1 variant is one contributing factor — this is the common genotype at the ankk1 taq1a marker
recommendations: Do not use this genotype to predict behavior or substance-use risk

AG.interpretation: One A1 allele has been linked to lower striatal dopamine D2/3 receptor availability in some healthy-control imaging studies
recommendations: Base behavioral or substance-use decisions on personal history and clinical assessment, not this marker

AA.interpretation: For taq1a, your ANKK1 variant is one contributing factor — two a1 alleles are uncommon
recommendations: Do not interpret this genotype as an addiction diagnosis or personality profile

sources.label: Taq1A and striatal dopamine receptor binding meta-analysis

limitations: Evidence for addiction and behavioral outcomes is inconsistent and not clinically predictive.
This locus is in ANKK1 and should not be presented as a deterministic DRD2 personality test.

CC.interpretation: For c-1019g, your HTR1A variant is one contributing factor — standard serotonin receptor 1a expression

CG.interpretation: For c-1019g, your HTR1A variant is one contributing factor — one copy - may affect antidepressant response
recommendations: May respond differently to some SSRIs

GG.interpretation: G allele linked to elevated risk of depression and suicide This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

3.interpretation: For u vntr, your MAOA variant is one contributing factor — 3-repeat allele - lower enzyme activity, less efficient dopamine/serotonin breakdown This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

4.interpretation: For u vntr, your MAOA variant is one contributing factor — 4-repeat allele - higher enzyme activity, efficient neurotransmitter breakdown

5.interpretation: For u vntr, your MAOA variant is one contributing factor — 5-repeat allele - highest activity, rapid neurotransmitter breakdown

3.5.interpretation: For u vntr, your MAOA variant is one contributing factor — intermediate activity

GT.interpretation: One copy - may have slightly lower brain serotonin
recommendations: Omega-3 and tryptophan support may help

TT.interpretation: linked to lower brain serotonin and mood disorder susceptibility This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

10/10.interpretation: For dat1 vntr, your SLC6A3 variant is one contributing factor — 10-repeat homozygote - standard dopamine reuptake

9/10.interpretation: One 9-copy allele - slightly elevated dopamine in synapse
recommendations: May have slightly higher attention span

9/9.interpretation: 9-repeat homozygote - lower dopamine reuptake, more dopamine in synapse This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

GG.interpretation: For a>g, your OXTR variant is one contributing factor — optimal oxytocin receptor sensitivity — enhanced empathy, social reward, and stress buffering from positive relationships; lower cortisol response to psychosocial stress
recommendations: Social connection is a key health lever for your genotype
Relationship quality strongly predicts health outcomes for GG carriers
Oxytocin-boosting activities: physical affection, group exercise, shared meals

AG.interpretation: For a>g, your OXTR variant is one contributing factor — moderate oxytocin receptor expression — standard social bonding with some stress buffering capacity
recommendations: Cultivate close social relationships as part of health strategy

AA.interpretation: lower oxytocin receptor sensitivity — less social buffering of stress, stronger cortisol response to social stressors, loneliness has a larger impact on health outcomes This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Actively cultivate close relationships and community — the health impact of loneliness is larger for AA carriers
Mindfulness and compassion practices may compensate for lower social buffering
Outdoor and group exercise more beneficial than solo training
AA.scienceSimplified: Your oxytocin system is wired to be less reactive to social signals, which means the stress-buffering effect of relationships is less automatic — you benefit most from deliberately building social infrastructure

CC.interpretation: For pro129thr (c385a), your FAAH variant is one contributing factor — standard endocannabinoid tone — normal anandamide breakdown, typical fear memory strength and stress reactivity
recommendations: Standard anxiety management: exercise, sleep, mindfulness

AC.interpretation: For pro129thr (c385a), your FAAH variant is one contributing factor — moderately elevated endocannabinoid levels — somewhat blunted stress response, easier fear extinction
recommendations: Likely lower baseline anxiety than CC carriers
CBD supplementation has less added effect at moderate doses

AA.effect: Markedly reduced FAAH activity (bliss molecule effect)
AA.interpretation: Persistently elevated anandamide (the 'bliss molecule') — notably lower anxiety, stronger natural fear extinction, more resilient stress response, better placebo response; linked to lower endocannabinoid-related pain
recommendations: Your endocannabinoid system already runs at high tone — CBD supplementation redundant at typical doses
Exercise amplifies this advantage (exercise-induced anandamide surge)
Focus on fear memory extinction through exposure to novelty and challenge
AA.scienceSimplified: Your FAAH enzyme breaks down anandamide slowly — anandamide is your body's natural cannabis-like molecule that reduces anxiety and fear. You have a naturally elevated baseline of it.

GG.effect: CRH receptor variant — altered HPA axis stress reactivity
GG.interpretation: Corticotropin-releasing hormone receptor 1 variant — linked to elevated risk for depression and anxiety under adversity; stress-sensitization phenotype; altered CRH receptor feedback This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Stress resilience training is especially high-ROI: regular mindfulness, HRV biofeedback
CRH system is calmed by exercise — 150+ min/week aerobic is the most potent HPA axis regulator
Cognitive behavioral therapy shown to be more effective in CRHR1 risk genotypes (biological basis for resilience building)
Avoid chronic stress exposure; prioritize psychological safety in work/relationships

AG.interpretation: For a>g, your CRHR1 variant is one contributing factor — one variant allele — moderate hpa axis stress reactivity
recommendations: Stress management practices and aerobic exercise beneficial

AA.interpretation: For a>g, your CRHR1 variant is one contributing factor — wild-type crh receptor — standard stress hormone feedback response

GG.effect: Higher norepinephrine transporter expression — more efficient NE reuptake
GG.interpretation: Faster norepinephrine clearance from synapses — lower noradrenergic tone; linked to lower reactivity to stress, calmer baseline state, potentially reduced ADHD symptoms
recommendations: Lower noradrenergic tone can mean lower alertness/drive in some contexts — aerobic exercise compensates
Caffeine and stimulant ergogenic effects may be reduced (lower NE target availability)

AG.interpretation: For a>g, your NET1 variant is one contributing factor — moderate norepinephrine reuptake efficiency

AA.effect: Lower NET1 expression — slower NE reuptake
AA.interpretation: Slower norepinephrine reuptake — elevated noradrenergic tone; linked to higher stress reactivity, better fight-or-flight response, and greater alertness/arousal
recommendations: Higher noradrenergic tone benefits from stress management training
HRV biofeedback and mindfulness directly downregulate NE system overactivation
